original_reasoning: Arabic luxury and well-being align with Greek 'much-nourishing' through a clear abundance and richness concept.
حظر is barrier, enclosure, and keeping apart; a temple keeper is one who guards the sacred boundary.
One who abstains from women points directly to the marriage-bed side of λέχος.
Piercing closely fits arrows and a quiver, preserving the core of perforating or pointed penetration.
The Arabic gloss explicitly mentions a spear, and αἰχμάς is accusative plural of spearheads; the concrete weapon sense is clearly shared.
Arabic qirdim resembles cardamom name; Greek = 'cardamom'. Strong loanword/etymological echo.
If tied to س ر د, the Arabic core is ordered narration or stringing together, which aligns well with more talkative or speaking.
Disordered or abusive speech on the Arabic side aligns clearly with Greek verbal utterance.
